findings.message: Plugin name is 'atm' but example invocations in command bodies use '/atm-scaffold' (hyphen); Claude Code registered paths would use 'atm:scaffold' (colon) or the bare name; inconsistent documentation confuses users.
MCP server declaration uses 'npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp' without a version pin; any malicious or breaking publish of the package silently affects all plugin users.
8 vague quantifiers in the ai skill; highest vague penalty of the two skill files; reduces instruction precision for commands that reference this skill for prompt-writing guidance.
6 vague quantifiers in bash skill (Always×3, graceful, informative, standard) without observable success criteria; reduces scoring precision for commands that load this skill.

APP_PATH set from $ARGUMENTS and passed to grep without sanitization; same pattern in angular-architecture-audit, angular-performance-audit, angular-style-audit, django-api-audit, django-model-audit (6 files total).
Expert persona commands (a11y-expert, angular-expert, django-expert) define no numbered process steps; the model has no deterministic execution order for multi-step tasks.
pbcopy used without OS detection fallback; command fails silently on non-macOS platforms; the bash skill documents a cross-platform clipboard abstraction that is not applied here.
$ARGUMENTS used without empty-input guard; -10 penalty combines with 8 vague quantifiers (-16) to produce score 19.
Python script written to /tmp/claude-filter.py and executed via subprocess in a git filter-repo callback; file is constructed by Claude and never persists between runs.
All 26 command files lack YAML frontmatter; name and description fields required for NLPM registration are absent; each file incurs -55 base penalty (name -25, description -25, allowed-tools -5).
Missing name (-25) and description (-25) in frontmatter across all 26 command files; allowed-tools also absent (-5); combined base penalty -55 per file applies to every command.

$ARGUMENTS consumed without empty check; worst-case file shown (spike-investigation scores 15); same defect in pr-review, feature-investigation, implement-pr-feedback, problem-solver, release-architect (6 files total).

All 26 command files lack YAML frontmatter; name and description fields required for NLPM registration are absent; each file incurs -55 base penalty (name -25, description -25, allowed-tools -5). — File begins with `# Scaffold New Project` (h1); no YAML frontmatter block found; pattern applies to all 26 command files.

Plugin name is 'atm' but example invocations in command bodies use '/atm-scaffold' (hyphen); Claude Code registered paths would use 'atm:scaffold' (colon) or the bare name; inconsistent documentation confuses users.
Python script written to /tmp/claude-filter.py and executed via subprocess in a git filter-repo callback; file is constructed by Claude and never persists between runs.

APP_PATH set from $ARGUMENTS and passed to grep without sanitization; same pattern in angular-architecture-audit, angular-performance-audit, angular-style-audit, django-api-audit, django-model-audit (6 files total).

MCP server declaration uses 'npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp' without a version pin; any malicious or breaking publish of the package silently affects all plugin users. — 'args': ['-y', '@upstash/context7-mcp'] — no version specifier; npx -y will install the latest published version on every MCP server start.

Missing name (-25) and description (-25) in frontmatter across all 26 command files; allowed-tools also absent (-5); combined base penalty -55 per file applies to every command. — File starts at line 1 with '# Scaffold New Project'; no YAML frontmatter delimiters found. Representative file; all 26 command files share this defect.

Expert persona commands (a11y-expert, angular-expert, django-expert) define no numbered process steps; the model has no deterministic execution order for multi-step tasks. — File body sets a persona ('You are now operating as a senior accessibility engineer...') then lists topics; no numbered steps or ordered workflow present. Same pattern in angular-expert.md and django-expert.md.

$ARGUMENTS consumed without empty check; worst-case file shown (spike-investigation scores 15); same defect in pr-review, feature-investigation, implement-pr-feedback, problem-solver, release-architect (6 files total). — SPIKE_TOPIC=$ARGUMENTS used at the top of the bash block with no preceding empty check; if invoked bare the variable is empty and SPIKE_ID derivation silently produces a malformed slug.

$ARGUMENTS used without empty-input guard; -10 penalty combines with 8 vague quantifiers (-16) to produce score 19. — FEATURE_ID=$ARGUMENTS assigned with no guard; downstream AC-gate step silently operates on empty string if invoked without arguments.

pbcopy used without OS detection fallback; command fails silently on non-macOS platforms; the bash skill documents a cross-platform clipboard abstraction that is not applied here. — 'pbcopy' appears in the step instruction for copying the commit message; pbcopy is a macOS-only command not available on Linux or Windows.

6 vague quantifiers in bash skill (Always×3, graceful, informative, standard) without observable success criteria; reduces scoring precision for commands that load this skill. — 'Always' appears 3 times as an unconditional directive; 'graceful', 'informative', 'standard' appear without measurable thresholds.

8 vague quantifiers in the ai skill; highest vague penalty of the two skill files; reduces instruction precision for commands that reference this skill for prompt-writing guidance. — effective, specific, appropriate, focused, relevant, clear, good, proper each appear at least once without measurable criteria.
